untranslatable_reason: Pattern identification requires visual recognition of 'twin peaks', 'narrow spires', 'hypodermic needle shape', and 'peaked vs rounded tops' — these are morphological features that cannot be encoded as mathematical indicator conditions without additional derived metrics (peak detection, curvature analysis, width-to-height ratios). The confirmation rule 'price closes below confirmation line' is translatable using pivot point as a proxy, but the core pattern detection is not achievable with the allowed indicators. Author also silent on stop placement and target computation.
translation_notes: AADT is a visual candlestick pattern requiring peak/trough detection and shape analysis. Used pivot point as a proxy for 'confirmation line' since exact confirmation line definition is not provided. Suggested stop_loss_pct of 2.0 based on failure_rate_5pct parameter. The 72% success rate and 19% average decline suggest a moderate-conviction SHORT setup, but pattern recognition itself requires bar-by-bar morphological analysis not available in this indicator set.
